mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 9% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals strong supply-side capacity utilization, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflation' risk premium, which depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in co-occurring inflation-rate environments are significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market demand, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 500 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, which reduces corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing demand (HOUST) indicates robust demand-side momentum, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
